_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: 17 Schools in Kelantan Flood Zones Damaged for 4th Consecutive Year
context: Annual monsoon flooding damaged 17 schools in Kelantan for the fourth year running affecting 12,000 students.

cards.text: 17 Kelantan schools flooded for the 4th consecutive year disrupting 12,000 students
cards.sub: Annual repair costs exceed RM15 million with no permanent flood mitigation

cards.h: Average learning time lost per affected student is 28 school days annually
cards.s: Cumulative loss over 4 years equals nearly half an academic year per student
cards.d: Average learning time lost per affected student is 28 school days annually Cumulative loss over 4 years equals nearly half an academic year per student

cards.h: MOE proposed relocating 8 schools to higher ground in 2022 but funding was not approved
cards.s: Relocation cost of RM120 million compares to RM60 million in recurring repairs
cards.d: MOE proposed relocating 8 schools to higher ground in 2022 but funding was not approved Relocation cost of RM120 million compares to RM60 million in recurring repairs

cards.h: Climate projections show Northeast monsoon intensity increasing 15% by 2035
cards.s: Flood frequency and severity will worsen without adaptation investment
cards.d: Climate projections show Northeast monsoon intensity increasing 15% by 2035 Flood frequency and severity will worsen without adaptation investment

cards.text: Rebuilding the same schools in the same flood zones every year is not resilience. It is denial.
Like mopping the same floor every day instead of fixing the leak in the ceiling. The mop budget grows but the floor never stays dry.
cards.sub: Annual rebuilding in known flood zones substitutes repetitive repair for permanent relocation or mitigation

cards.text: Should flood-prone schools be permanently relocated even if it costs more upfront

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
